title: Views, Triggers, Reusable Database Logic, and Boundaries
description: Multiple applications need a stable read contract and audited state transitions without hidden business behavior. Learners cumulatively produce a versioned read model and audited transition design through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy.
Choose live views, materialized views, summary tables, and cache refresh strategies from freshness needs.
Predict trigger timing, row versus statement scope, recursion, ordering limits, and failure behavior.
